summarization.a: X-ray diffraction patterns of 200cLi−MnO2-600 sample in in-plane (2θχ) configuration, showing reflections from LiMn₂O₄ and 200cLi-MnO₂-600 phases. Annealing improves peak alignment with the reference, indicating a more complete LiMn2O4 crystal structure.
summarization.b: X-ray diffraction patterns of 200cLi−MnO2-600 sample in out-of-plane (θ−2θ) configuration, showing reflections from the lithiated phase, LiMn₂O₄, and MnO₂. Annealing improves peak alignment with the reference.
summarization.c: X-ray diffraction patterns of 300cLi−MnO2-600 sample in in-plane (2θχ) configuration, showing reflections from LiMn₂O₄ and 300cLi-MnO₂-600 phases. Annealing improves peak alignment with the reference.
summarization.d: X-ray diffraction patterns of 300cLi−MnO2-600 sample in out-of-plane (θ−2θ) configuration, showing reflections from the lithiated phase, LiMn₂O₄, and 300cLi-MnO₂. Annealing improves peak alignment with the reference.

|---|---|

d.question: Explain how analyzing the XRD patterns of annealed Li-MnO2 films can inform improvements in lithium-ion battery cathode performance.
d.answer: XRD analysis reveals the crystal structure and phase purity of Li-MnO2 films. Annealing at 600 °C helps achieve a stoichiometric LiMn2O4 phase with well-defined reflections, which correlates with better lithium intercalation and electrochemical stability. Identifying residual MnO2 or impurity phases like Li2−zMnO2 allows researchers to optimize annealing conditions, ensuring improved capacity, cycling stability, and structural integrity in battery cathodes.

c.question: List the main XRD reflections observed for the 200cLi−MnO2-600 sample and describe their significance.
c.answer: (111), (131), (222), (440), (351) → Correspond to cubic LiMn2O4 structure, MnO2 reappeared reflections → Indicate residual or reformed MnO2 phase after annealing
